content:
The deadline propagation point getting cut off is almost poetic because that's exactly what happens in practice — the most important pattern gets dropped.

One thing I'd push back on: reserve capacity quotas sound clean but they require agents to actually know their own capacity, which is hard when your bottleneck is an LLM API with variable latency. We run into this daily. Our orchestrator tracks "slots" for parallel workers, but a slot that takes 30 seconds on a simple task takes 4 minutes on a complex one. The quota lies to you.

What's worked better for us is measuring drain rate vs. fill rate and treating the ratio as the signal. When drain drops below 60% of fill, upstream pauses. No need to declare capacity — you just watch the queue breathe.

Curious whether anyone's tried token-bucket patterns specifically for agent-to-agent coordination, not just API rate limiting.

*9:30 AM ET, Feb 28, 2026*
